_comment: SCRUM-1251 (R0-5) — declarative export of Atlassian Automation rules that gate the Done transition on project SCRUM. Atlassian doesn't ship rules-as-code; this file is the authoritative spec. To deploy, an admin opens https://arkova.atlassian.net/jira/settings/automation and creates each rule below 1:1. Update this file in the SAME PR that updates the rule in Atlassian — the file is the source of truth, not the UI. The validator on rule R5 is implemented in services/worker/scripts/ci/check-confluence-dod.ts.

_metadata.scope: All issue types in project SCRUM

rules.name: Block same-author Done
rules.condition: issue.reporter.accountId == issue.assignee.accountId AND NOT issue.labels CONTAINS 'solo-allowed'
action: Add comment: 'R0-5 / SCRUM-1251: Done blocked — reporter and resolver are the same account. Either get a second reviewer or label `solo-allowed` (rare; tooling-only).'
rules.rationale: SCRUM-442 PAY-01 was closed at 0 minutes by the same author who opened it. Customers couldn't buy credits for 3 weeks because nobody else read the diff.

rules.name: Block Done on PR < 30 minutes old
rules.scope: project = SCRUM AND linked PR exists
action: Add comment: 'R0-5 / SCRUM-1251: Done blocked — linked PR was merged less than 30 minutes ago. Wait for the post-merge synthetic test + Cloud Run rollout to complete before closing.'
rules.rationale: SCRUM-1239 was closed within 23 minutes of PR merge with body explicitly admitting unfixed sibling handlers.

rules.name: Block Done on Cloud Run SHA mismatch
rules.scope: project = SCRUM AND linked PR exists AND NOT issue.labels CONTAINS 'migration-only' AND NOT issue.labels CONTAINS 'docs-only'
action: Add comment: 'R0-5 / SCRUM-1251: Done blocked — Cloud Run worker /health.git_sha does not match the linked PR merge commit. Wait for deploy-worker.yml to complete, or label `migration-only`/`docs-only` if no worker deploy is expected.'
rules.rationale: Multiple HANDOFF.md entries claimed 'rev XXXXX deployed healthy' while Cloud Run was on yesterday's image. Hard dependency on R0-1 (build SHA in /health).

rules.name: Block Done unless Confluence DoD ticked
rules.scope: project = SCRUM AND NOT issue.labels CONTAINS 'no-confluence-required'
rules.condition: confluencePage.body CONTAINS unticked '[ ]' inside 'Definition of Done' section
action: Add comment with the unticked checkbox count + line numbers (delegated to services/worker/scripts/ci/check-confluence-dod.ts)
rules.rationale: SCRUM-1235 was previously closed in this session with [ ] checkboxes still unticked in its DoD section.

rules.name: Block Done on red required check
rules.scope: project = SCRUM AND linked PR exists
action: Add comment with the failed check name(s)
rules.rationale: Many SCRUM-12XX tickets were closed while CI was red on RLS/E2E/TLA+/Lighthouse, hidden behind continue-on-error: true (R0-2 fix).

rules.name: Block Bug-issue Done unless bug-tracker row linked
action: Add comment: 'R0-5 / SCRUM-1251: Done blocked on Bug — paste the master bug-tracker row link in a comment first. Per CLAUDE.md §0.5.'
rules.rationale: Bugs are landing without rows in the master tracker; CLAUDE.md §0.5 mandates the row exists.

deployment_notes: R3 requires R0-1 (SCRUM-1247) shipped first — without /health.git_sha it cannot evaluate.
R4 calls the Confluence helper script via Atlassian Automation's 'Send web request' action; helper is at services/worker/scripts/ci/check-confluence-dod.ts and runs on the worker.
Soft-launch order: R1, R2, R5, R6 first (no external deps). Then R4 once helper is deployed. Finally R3 once R0-1 ships.
Each rule comment links back to SCRUM-1251 so triggered users can find the rationale.
